title: Pasta network with rootless Traefik container fails after period of time
body:
### Issue Description

Running Traefik reverse proxy using the pasta network mode will fail after some use. Sometimes after a couple of days, sometimes as quickly as a few minutes. The only thing I can find in the journal is:

```
passt.avx2[1058745]:  Failed to open tun socket in namespace
```

The error seems to occur most frequently when I'm streaming media from a Jellyfin container. Restarting the `systemd` unit for Traefik usually fixes the issue for another period of time. I did not have this issue with the slirp4netns network mode. I get no error logs at all within Traefik itself, but every web service becomes unreachable.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run Traefik rootless via podman-compose:
```
services:
  traefik:
    image: docker.io/library/traefik:2
    container_name: traefik
    network_mode: "pasta"
    ports:
      - 80:80
      - 443:443
```
2. Use Traefik's "file provider" method (not via exposing the podman socket) to connect to Jellyfin (bound to an internal dummy loopback).

3. Stream media from Jellyfin (music or movies, doesn't seem to matter).


### Describe the results you received

All containers behind the reverse proxy are unreachable (although still reachable via their IP address).

### Describe the results you expected

Traefik to not crash all web services.

### Additional information

comments.body:
I suggest you first try to update podman and pasta to the latest version, at the very least the latest pasta should include a much better error for that condition (https://passt.top/passt/commit/?id=7bc9b66fc2fae6b81ce497a34a5692f63691c810)

`Failed to open tun socket in namespace`, as best I can tell this can only happen on startup and should not happen after some time so that would be weird.
It would also help if you could define what fails mean? Is the process gone, does the interface still exists in the container namespace, are the ip address still there, etc...

Thank you for the reply.

I apologize for not being more specific. It's generally been crashing during moments when I don't have the time to investigate further. By fail I just meant that no services could be resolved through the reverse proxy. No gateway timeouts, nothing. Which leads me to believe it *could* have been a problem with the network interface.

The container's process still existed, and I was able to interact with it via `podman exec` and look at the logs via `podman logs`, but unable to access anything through the networked ports.

I have installed the latest (https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/sbrivio/passt/build/6387922/) build of pasta, and will report back after testing. Previously was the most updated available in the repo (https://almalinux.pkgs.org/9/almalinux-appstream-x86_64/passt-0%5E20230222.g4ddbcb9-4.el9_2.x86_64.rpm.html).
After two weeks now, I haven't had a single crash. Still using the same version as posted in the above comment. Thank you @Luap99 for the tip. Closing the issue.
